notes.abortion: Pro-choice Republican. Criticized Dobbs decision, said overturning Roe v. Wade 'would be an enormous step backwards and damage civil rights'
notes.guns: Signed gun control bills after Parkland shooting, earning NRA F rating - notable for a Republican
notes.climate: First VT governor with all-electric official vehicle
notes.moderate: Described as liberal Republican, wins in deep blue state by being socially progressive

profile.background: Construction company owner and semi-professional stock car racer; Vermont state senator (2001-2011); lieutenant governor (2011-2017); became 82nd governor in 2017.

notes.self_described_socialist: Democratic socialist, longest-serving independent in US congressional history
notes.income_inequality: Primary focus on income inequality and taxing the wealthy
notes.climate: Supports Green New Deal-style climate legislation
notes.committees: Chaired Senate HELP Committee (2023-2025), Veterans Affairs (2013-2015), Budget (2021-2023)

profile.background: Longest-serving independent in congressional history. Former Mayor of Burlington, Vermont. Self-described democratic socialist. 2016 and 2020 presidential candidate. Former Budget Committee chair.

notes.climate: Believes climate change is a 'glaring problem'
notes.education: Challenged wealthy university endowments to spend more on operations (with Sen. Grassley)
notes.immigration: Supports immigration reform, opposes border closures
notes.progressive: Generally progressive voting record aligned with Vermont values

profile.religion: Catholic (attended College of the Holy Cross, Jesuit institution)
profile.education: College of the Holy Cross (BA), UC Berkeley School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former U.S. House member and Vermont Senate President. Public defender turned personal injury lawyer. Succeeded Bernie Sanders in House. Oldest freshman senator when elected at 75.
profile.heritage_action: {'lifetime': '~5 (estimated based on pattern, first Senate term)', 'house_record': 'Consistently low as House member'}

notes.historic: First woman and first openly LGBTQ person to represent Vermont in Congress
notes.progressive_caucus: Leader in the Congressional Progressive Caucus
notes.democracy: Outspoken defender of democracy and civil rights
notes.lgbtq: Identifies as LGBTQ, strong advocate for LGBTQ rights

profile.education: Smith College, Harvard Graduate School of Education
